Mixed Greens Salad with Walnuts, Pomegranate and Goat Cheese / Kale and Quinoa Salad with Lemon-Tahini Dressing
Oyster Po’ Boy with Avocado Slaw / Grilled Lamb Chops and Roasted Asparagus
Watermelon and Maca Smoothie / Pomegranate Performance Juice / Ashwagandha Tea
In addition to your meal-specific smoothie, aim for the following each day across all 8 weeks:
One performance shot in the morning before eating. Rotate between the options in Chapter 6 to prevent boredom.
One fruit-infused performance water throughout the day, minimum 1.5 liters. Prep it the night before.

Green tea before noon. One to two cups. It improves blood flow through catechin flavonoids, provides calm focused energy through L-theanine, and costs almost nothing. This is one of the easiest daily habits in the entire plan.

The Sleep Optimizer smoothie at least three evenings per week, particularly on training days. Tart cherry and magnesium before bed measurably improves sleep depth and testosterone production overnight.
Minimum 80 ounces of (additional) plain water daily, not counting your infused waters and smoothies. Use your glass water bottle as a tracking tool.

Stock these items before Day 1. Prioritize organic where budget allows, particularly for produce items you eat daily. Bold items are highest priority and should be in your kitchen at all times throughout the 8 weeks.
Grass-fed ground beef or bison, 2 lbs
Grass-fed steak cuts such as ribeye or sirloin, 1 to 2 lbs
Wild-caught salmon fillets, 2 lbs fresh or frozen
Canned oysters, 2 tins, or 6 fresh oysters
Large shrimp, peeled and deveined, 1 lb
Sardines in olive oil, 3 to 4 tins
Baby spinach and kale, large bags
Fresh turmeric root or ground turmeric
Pomegranate juice, 100 percent pure, 1 large bottle
Lemons and limes, 4 to 6 of each
Fresh strawberries, 1 pint (for Spinach and Strawberry Salad)
Pomegranate seeds, 1/2 cup fresh or use pomegranate juice already listed
Raw honey and apple cider vinegar
Dark chocolate 70 percent cacao or higher
Raw cacao powder and maca root powder
Cinnamon, cayenne, paprika, cumin, black pepper
Coconut aminos or low-sodium soy sauce
Unsweetened almond milk or coconut milk
Rolled oats, 1 bag (for Oatmeal with Flaxseed, Banana and Honey)
Tahini, 1 jar (for Kale and Quinoa Salad dressing)
Canned green or brown lentils, 2 tins (for Lentil and Spinach Stew)
Coconut water, 1 liter (for Coconut Water Electrolyte Drink)
Goat cheese, 4 oz (for Mixed Greens Salad with Pomegranate and Walnuts)
Low-sodium soy sauce, 1 bottle (if not using coconut aminos)
Ashwagandha KSM-66 powder (for Ashwagandha Tea, in addition to capsules if on the stack)
Feta cheese and Greek yogurt full-fat
After completing the 7-day starter plan, the Meal Maker tool in the Perfect Stack app generates personalized daily meals for the remaining weeks of your protocol.
The 8-week framework follows four performance phases, each emphasizing different nutritional priorities:
Weeks 1-2 (Foundation and Detox): Anti-inflammatory foods, clean protein sources, and gut-supportive ingredients establish the baseline. The 7-day starter plan covers this phase.
Weeks 3-4 (Nitric Oxide Load): Recipes emphasize nitrate-rich vegetables, L-Citrulline sources, and omega-3 fatty acids to maximize blood flow and vascular performance.
Weeks 5-6 (Testosterone Support): Meals prioritize zinc-dense proteins, healthy fats, and testosterone-supportive micronutrients including selenium, magnesium, and vitamin D food sources.
Weeks 7-8 (Optimization and Habit Lock): The full recipe library is available. Meal selection reinforces the habits established in earlier phases while maintaining all nutritional targets.

Each day follows one of seven rotating themes: Energize and Circulate, Strengthen and Build, Anti-Inflammatory Reset, Testosterone Focus, Vascular Performance, Longevity and Recovery, and Full Protocol Day, which cycle continuously regardless of which week you are in.
Open the Recipes and Meal Plan page in the app
The app automatically shows the next unplanned day with the correct week phase and daily theme pre-populated
Tap Generate to receive a full day of meals - breakfast, lunch, dinner, drink, and smoothie - selected to match your current phase and theme while avoiding recent repeats
Each generated meal links to the full recipe with photo, ingredients, and instructions
Foundation subscribers receive a free Day 8 preview. The full 7-week generator (Days 8 through 56) is available in the Ultimate Protocol.
Access Meal Maker at getperfectstack.com under Recipes and Meal Plan.

Every recipe in this section is built around the performance foods from Chapter 4. They're not complicated. They're not restaurant food. They're practical, deliciously satisfying meals and drinks that stack the nutritional deck in your favor every time you make them. Prep times are realistic for a busy schedule, and most ingredients are easy to find.
The shots and smoothies are designed to be part of a daily or weekly routine, not one-time experiments. Pick the ones that fit your schedule and stick with them.

The Recipes section of the Perfect Stack app at getperfectstack.com includes every recipe in this guide with photos, the 7-Day Starter Plan, the shopping lists, and the Meal Maker tool that automatically builds personalized daily meals for the remaining 2 to 8 weeks of the program.
6 oz ground grass-fed beef or bison
Salt, black pepper, garlic powder to taste
Brown the beef in olive oil over medium heat. Season with salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
In the same pan, push the beef aside and crack the eggs in. Cook to your preference.
Warm the quinoa and arrange in a bowl with spinach as the base.
Top with the beef, eggs, and avocado. Finish with a drizzle of olive oil.

Beef and eggs provide zinc, cholesterol for testosterone synthesis, and complete amino acids. Spinach delivers magnesium and nitrates. Avocado adds monounsaturated fats that support hormone production.
Seared Salmon with Beet and Arugula Salad
Season salmon with salt and pepper. Sear in a hot pan with a teaspoon of olive oil, about 4 minutes per side.
Whisk together remaining olive oil, lemon juice, and Dijon for the dressing.
Toss arugula, beets, and walnuts with the dressing.
Plate the salad and top with the salmon.
Salmon delivers omega-3s and vitamin D. Beets and arugula are nitrate powerhouses. Walnuts provide arginine. This plate hits almost every vascular support nutrient in one shot.
Garlic Butter Steak with Roasted Asparagus
Bring steak to room temperature. Season generously with salt and pepper.
Sear steak in olive oil over high heat, 3 to 4 minutes per side for medium-rare. Rest for 5 minutes.
In the same pan, reduce heat and add butter, garlic, and herbs. Spoon over the resting steak.
Roast asparagus at 400 degrees with olive oil, salt, and pepper for 12 to 15 minutes.

Red meat is one of the most bioavailable sources of zinc and heme iron. Garlic contains allicin, which studies link to reduced cortisol and improved testosterone. Asparagus provides folate and potassium for vascular health.
1 can whole oysters or 6 fresh oysters
8 oz large shrimp, peeled and deveined
2 tbsp coconut aminos or low-sodium soy sauce
Heat sesame oil in a wok or large pan over high heat.
Add shrimp and cook 2 minutes per side. Remove and set aside.
Add garlic, ginger, broccoli, and peppers. Stir-fry 4 minutes.
Add oysters and shrimp back in with coconut aminos and chili flakes. Toss and serve over brown rice.

Oysters are the richest dietary source of zinc on earth. A single serving can exceed your daily zinc requirement. Zinc is directly required for testosterone synthesis, sperm production, and immune function.
2 large bell peppers, halved and seeded
Brown turkey with garlic, cumin, and paprika. Mix in tomatoes, beans, and quinoa.
Fill pepper halves with the mixture and place in a baking dish with a splash of water.
Bake 25 to 30 minutes. Top with cheese in the last 5 minutes if using. Garnish with cilantro.
A lean, high-protein meal with balanced macronutrients. Quinoa provides complete protein and magnesium. Black beans add fiber to support gut health and estrogen clearance.
Sardines on Whole Grain Toast with Tomato and Capers
1 tin wild sardines in olive oil
2 slices whole grain or sourdough bread
Drain sardines lightly and arrange on toast.
Top with tomato slices, capers, a squeeze of lemon, and a drizzle of olive oil.
Finish with cracked black pepper and fresh parsley.

Sardines are one of the most nutrient-dense foods available. They deliver omega-3s, vitamin D, B12, selenium, and calcium. Lycopene in tomatoes supports prostate health. This is a five-minute performance meal that punches far above its weight.
Sauté garlic and spinach in olive oil in an oven-safe skillet until wilted.
Whisk eggs with salt, pepper, and oregano. Pour over the spinach.
Add tomatoes and feta on top. Cook on stovetop 2 minutes then transfer to oven for 12 to 15 minutes until set.

Eggs are among the most complete foods for male hormone health. The yolk contains cholesterol for testosterone production, choline for brain health, and fat-soluble vitamins. Spinach adds magnesium and dietary nitrates.
Grilled Chicken Thighs with Pomegranate Glaze
Mix pomegranate juice, honey, vinegar, garlic, and paprika for the glaze.
Season chicken with salt and pepper. Grill over medium-high heat 7 to 8 minutes per side.
Brush with glaze in the last 5 minutes of cooking.
Rest 5 minutes before serving. Garnish with mint.

Pomegranate has some of the strongest clinical evidence for male sexual health of any food. Studies show it measurably increases testosterone and improves blood flow. Pairing it with protein makes this a complete performance meal.
Lamb Chops with Garlic Roasted Vegetables
1 cup mixed root vegetables (parsnip, sweet potato, beet)
Toss vegetables and garlic with olive oil, herbs, salt, and pepper. Roast at 400 degrees for 25 minutes.
Season lamb chops generously. Rub with harissa if using.
Sear in a hot cast iron pan 3 to 4 minutes per side for medium.
Rest 5 minutes and serve alongside roasted vegetables.
Lamb is one of the richest animal sources of zinc and is higher in omega-3s than conventional beef. It also provides carnitine, which supports fat metabolism and mitochondrial energy production.
2 cans wild albacore tuna in water, drained
Large romaine or butter lettuce leaves
Mash avocado with lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Mix in tuna, celery, and capers. Season with paprika.
Spoon into lettuce leaves and serve immediately.
A clean, fast, high-protein meal with omega-3s from tuna and monounsaturated fats from avocado. Low carb, anti-inflammatory, and ready in minutes.
Herb-Roasted Chicken with Sweet Potato and Greens
2 cups mixed greens (kale, spinach, or arugula)
Preheat oven to 400 degrees. Toss sweet potato cubes with 1 tbsp olive oil, paprika, salt, and pepper. Spread on a baking sheet.
Rub chicken thighs with remaining olive oil, garlic, thyme, salt, and pepper. Nestle alongside the sweet potatoes.
Roast 30 to 35 minutes until chicken skin is golden and internal temperature reaches 165 degrees. Rest 5 minutes.
While chicken rests, quickly sauté greens in the pan drippings over medium heat until just wilted, about 2 minutes. Finish with lemon juice.
Plate sweet potato, greens, and chicken together.

Sweet potato provides beta-carotene, potassium, and slow-digesting carbohydrates that support sustained energy and cortisol control. Chicken thighs deliver zinc and protein. The dark leafy greens add dietary nitrates and magnesium, covering blood flow and hormone support in a single plate.
Oatmeal with Flaxseed, Banana and Honey
1 cup water or unsweetened almond milk
Cook oats in water or almond milk according to package instructions.
Top with banana slices and drizzle honey.

Oats contain avenanthramides, a class of antioxidants shown to support healthy blood pressure and nitric oxide function. Flaxseed provides plant-based omega-3 fatty acids and lignans that support testosterone by modulating estrogen metabolism. Banana delivers potassium for circulation and magnesium for smooth muscle function. A clean, high-fiber breakfast that sets a strong hormonal tone before noon.
Garlic and Chili Shrimp with Brown Rice
1 cup cooked brown rice (per package instructions)
Salt and black pepper to taste
Heat olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat.
Add garlic and chili flakes. Sauté until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add shrimp and cook until pink and opaque, about 3 minutes per side.

Shrimp is one of the most zinc-dense foods available, and zinc is directly required for testosterone synthesis and sperm production. Garlic contains allicin, which studies link to improved blood flow and reduced cortisol. Chili flakes contain capsaicin, which improves circulation and has mild testosterone-supportive effects. Brown rice provides magnesium and slow-digesting carbohydrates for sustained energy without insulin spikes.
Salt and black pepper to taste
Brown ground beef with onion and garlic in a heavy pot over medium heat.
Add diced tomatoes, chili powder, cumin, salt, and pepper.
Simmer uncovered for 20 to 25 minutes until flavors develop.
Stir in pumpkin seeds just before serving.

Lean ground beef is one of the most bioavailable sources of zinc and heme iron. Pumpkin seeds add a second zinc source along with plant-based arginine that supports nitric oxide production. Chili powder improves circulation through capsaicin, and the tomato base provides lycopene for prostate and vascular health. This is a high-zinc, high-protein meal that covers multiple testosterone and blood flow pathways in a single dish.
Lentil and Spinach Stew with Turmeric
1 cup cooked green or brown lentils (per package instructions)
Heat olive oil in a pot over medium heat.
Sauté onion and garlic until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Add turmeric and cumin. Stir 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add cooked lentils and spinach. Stir to combine.
Simmer 10 minutes until flavors meld. Season with salt and serve warm.

Lentils provide folate, plant protein, and slow-digesting carbohydrates that support energy and hormone metabolism. Spinach delivers dietary nitrates for blood flow and magnesium for testosterone production and sleep quality. Turmeric with olive oil provides curcumin, one of the most studied anti-inflammatory compounds available. Fat from the olive oil is required for curcumin absorption. This is a plant-based performance meal that delivers meaningful nutritional density without relying on animal protein.
Spinach and Strawberry Salad with Grilled Chicken
Season chicken with salt and pepper. Grill over medium-high heat 5 to 6 minutes per side until cooked through. Let rest 3 minutes, then slice.
Whisk olive oil and balsamic vinegar for the dressing.
Top salad with chicken slices, almonds, and dressing. Serve immediately.

Strawberry flavonoids support endothelial function and have been shown to improve blood flow markers and reduce oxidative stress in blood vessels. Spinach adds dietary nitrates for nitric oxide production. Chicken breast delivers complete protein and L-arginine, the substrate your body uses to build nitric oxide. Almonds provide zinc and vitamin E. This plate hits nitric oxide support, endothelial protection, and complete protein in a single simple meal.
Kale and Quinoa Salad with Lemon-Tahini Dressing
Massage kale with a squeeze of lemon juice for 1 to 2 minutes to soften the leaves.
Toss kale with quinoa, almonds, and cherry tomatoes.
Whisk together tahini, remaining lemon juice, honey, and water until smooth.
Drizzle dressing over salad, toss gently, and serve.

Kale is among the highest dietary sources of nitrates and also provides vitamin K for cardiovascular health and antioxidants that protect blood vessel walls. Quinoa delivers complete protein and magnesium, covering both muscle recovery and testosterone support. Tahini provides zinc and healthy monounsaturated fat for hormone production. The lemon dressing adds vitamin C, which protects nitric oxide from oxidative breakdown and extends its active window in the body.
Mixed Greens Salad with Walnuts, Pomegranate and Goat Cheese
Toss mixed greens, walnuts, and pomegranate seeds in a bowl.
Crumble goat cheese over the top.
Whisk olive oil and balsamic vinegar together and drizzle over the salad.

Pomegranate is one of the most clinically studied foods for male sexual health. Multiple human trials show it measurably increases testosterone and improves blood flow markers. Walnuts provide arginine and plant-based omega-3s that support nitric oxide synthesis and vascular flexibility. Goat cheese adds protein and calcium. Mixed greens contribute dietary nitrates. This is a fast, complete plate that stacks antioxidant protection, testosterone support, and blood flow nutrients together.
Greek Yogurt with Pomegranate and Walnuts
Layer Greek yogurt, pomegranate seeds, and walnuts in a bowl.

Pomegranate has clinical evidence for increasing free testosterone and improving blood flow in men. Walnuts provide arginine and omega-3s that support the nitric oxide pathway. Greek yogurt delivers complete protein and probiotics that support gut health and estrogen metabolism. A healthy gut microbiome helps clear excess estrogen, which keeps the testosterone-to-estrogen ratio favorable. This is a high-impact, no-cook breakfast or snack that covers multiple performance pathways.
Avocado and Tomato Whole Grain Toast
1 slice whole grain or sourdough bread
Salt and black pepper to taste
Mash avocado and spread evenly over the toast.
Drizzle olive oil and season with salt and pepper.

Avocado is one of the best dietary sources of monounsaturated fat, which research consistently links to higher testosterone levels. The fat also supports absorption of fat-soluble vitamins including vitamin D. Tomatoes provide lycopene, a powerful antioxidant that supports prostate health and protects blood vessel walls from oxidative damage. Olive oil contributes its own monounsaturated fat and polyphenols that support vascular function. A fast, clean breakfast that supports testosterone synthesis and vascular health from the first meal of the day.
Grilled Salmon Salad with Beets and Walnuts
Season salmon with salt and pepper. Grill over medium-high heat 4 minutes per side until cooked through.
Toss mixed greens and roasted beets with olive oil and balsamic vinegar.
Top salad with grilled salmon and walnuts.

Salmon delivers omega-3 fatty acids that reduce systemic inflammation and support cardiovascular health, along with vitamin D, which is directly required for testosterone production. Beets provide dietary nitrates that convert to nitric oxide in the body, improving blood flow to working muscle and erectile tissue. Walnuts add arginine for additional nitric oxide support and additional omega-3s for vascular flexibility. This plate works on two distinct blood flow pathways simultaneously. Pair it with the NO Rocket smoothie on the same day for a full nitrate and omega-3 stack effect.
Garlic and Herb Roasted Chicken with Broccoli
Salt and black pepper to taste
Toss chicken with olive oil, garlic, rosemary, salt, and pepper.
Arrange chicken and broccoli on a baking sheet in a single layer.
Roast 25 to 30 minutes until chicken is cooked through and broccoli edges are slightly charred.

Garlic is one of the most studied foods for male hormone health. The allicin compounds in garlic have been shown in multiple studies to reduce cortisol and increase testosterone by lowering the stress-hormone interference that suppresses T production. The mechanisms for this are covered in Chapter 4. Broccoli provides indole-3-carbinol and DIM, cruciferous compounds that support healthy estrogen metabolism and help maintain a favorable testosterone-to-estrogen ratio. Rosemary contributes antioxidant polyphenols that protect against oxidative stress in testosterone-producing cells.
Prep/Cook: 15 min | Servings: 1 | Phase: Weeks 1-2, Foundation
2 oz cooked ground turkey or lean beef
Scramble eggs in a lightly oiled pan over medium heat until just set.
Warm tortilla in a dry pan or microwave for 15 seconds.
Layer eggs, ground meat, black beans, avocado, salsa, and cheese on the tortilla.
Fold sides in and roll tightly into a burrito.

Whole eggs deliver cholesterol for testosterone synthesis, choline for brain health, and complete amino acids. Black beans add fiber and zinc. Avocado provides monounsaturated fats that directly support hormone production. Ground turkey or beef adds additional zinc and creatine. A fast, portable breakfast that covers the core nutritional pillars of the Foundation phase.
Prep/Cook: 10 min | Servings: 1 | Phase: Weeks 1-2, Foundation
Spread mashed avocado evenly on the toast.
Layer smoked salmon on top of the avocado.
Add capers, a squeeze of lemon juice, cracked pepper, and fresh dill.

Smoked salmon delivers omega-3 fatty acids and vitamin D, both of which are directly linked to testosterone production and vascular health in clinical research. Avocado adds monounsaturated fats that support hormone synthesis. The combination of omega-3s and healthy fats makes this one of the most efficient testosterone-supporting breakfasts available with zero cooking required.
Prep/Cook: 25 min | Servings: 1 | Phase: Weeks 3-4, Nitric Oxide Load
Heat olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat.
Sauté garlic until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add shrimp and cook 2 minutes per side until pink and opaque.
Toss spinach into the pan and let it wilt for 30 seconds.
Serve shrimp and spinach over quinoa, topped with cherry tomatoes and lemon juice.

Shrimp is one of the most zinc-dense proteins available, and zinc is directly required for testosterone synthesis. Garlic contains allicin, which supports nitric oxide production and reduces cortisol. Quinoa delivers complete protein and magnesium for testosterone bioavailability. Spinach adds dietary nitrates for the NO pathway. This bowl stacks multiple vascular and hormone support nutrients into a single plate.
Prep/Cook: 20 min | Servings: 2 | Phase: Weeks 3-4, Nitric Oxide Load
8 oz flank steak or sirloin, sliced thin
1 cup cooked brown rice (for serving, per package instructions)
Heat olive oil in a wok or large pan over high heat.
Add beef slices and sear for 2 minutes until browned. Remove and set aside.
Add broccoli, garlic, and ginger to the pan. Stir-fry 3 to 4 minutes until broccoli is tender-crisp.
Return beef to the pan. Add soy sauce and sesame oil, toss to combine.

Beef provides zinc, iron, creatine, and complete amino acids that directly support testosterone production and muscle recovery. Broccoli delivers indole-3-carbinol and DIM, cruciferous compounds that promote healthy estrogen metabolism and help maintain a favorable testosterone-to-estrogen ratio. Garlic and ginger both support nitric oxide production and circulation.
Prep/Cook: 10 min | Servings: 1 | Phase: Weeks 1-2, Foundation
Combine tuna, olives, cucumber, tomatoes, and red onion in a bowl.
Drizzle olive oil and lemon juice over the mixture and toss gently.
Serve over a bed of mixed greens.

Wild-caught tuna delivers omega-3 fatty acids, selenium, and high-quality protein with minimal preparation. Olives provide monounsaturated fats and polyphenols that support cardiovascular health. The Mediterranean diet pattern has some of the strongest clinical evidence for improving endothelial function and blood flow. A zero-cook, high-protein lunch that fits easily into the Foundation phase.
Grilled Lamb Chops and Roasted Asparagus
Prep/Cook: 30 min | Servings: 2 | Phase: Weeks 5-6, Peak Performance
Salt and cracked black pepper to taste
Preheat grill to medium-high or oven to 425 degrees.
Rub lamb chops with 1 tbsp olive oil, garlic, rosemary, thyme, salt, and pepper.
Toss asparagus with remaining olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Grill lamb chops 4 minutes per side for medium-rare, or adjust to preference.
Roast asparagus in the oven for 12 to 15 minutes or grill alongside the lamb.
Rest lamb 5 minutes before serving alongside asparagus.

Lamb is one of the richest animal sources of zinc and is higher in omega-3 fatty acids than conventional beef. It also provides carnitine, which supports fat metabolism and mitochondrial energy production. Asparagus delivers folate and saponins, both of which support testosterone production. This is a peak-phase meal designed for men who have built their nutritional foundation and are ready to maximize performance output.
Prep/Cook: 20 min | Servings: 2 | Phase: Weeks 3-4, Nitric Oxide Load
Cook pasta according to package instructions. Reserve 1/2 cup pasta water before draining.
Heat olive oil in a large pan. Sauté garlic until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add spinach and cook until just wilted, about 2 minutes.
Toss drained pasta into the pan with spinach. Add pumpkin seeds, lemon juice, and red pepper flakes.
Add pasta water as needed for moisture. Top with parmesan, salt, and pepper.

Pumpkin seeds are one of the richest plant sources of zinc, delivering roughly 2.5 mg per ounce, and zinc is directly required for testosterone synthesis and sperm production. Spinach provides dietary nitrates that convert to nitric oxide for improved blood flow. Whole wheat pasta supplies B vitamins and sustained energy. The combination of zinc and nitrates makes this an effective NO-phase meal for men who prefer plant-forward options.
Oyster Po’ Boy with Avocado Slaw
Prep/Cook: 25 min | Servings: 2 | Phase: Weeks 5-6, Peak Performance
12 fresh oysters, shucked (or 1 can whole oysters, drained)
Mix cornmeal, flour, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and pepper in a bowl.
Coat each oyster in the cornmeal mixture.
Heat olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Pan-fry oysters 2 minutes per side until golden and crispy.
Toss cabbage, diced avocado, and lime juice to make the slaw.
Split rolls, layer oysters inside, and top with avocado slaw.

Oysters are the single richest dietary source of zinc on earth. A single serving can exceed your daily zinc requirement several times over, and zinc is directly required for testosterone synthesis, sperm production, and immune function. Avocado adds monounsaturated fats for hormone support. This is a peak-phase meal that delivers the most potent zinc dose available from whole food in a satisfying, restaurant-quality format.
Black Bean and Egg Breakfast Skillet
Prep/Cook: 15 min | Servings: 1 | Phase: Weeks 1-2, Foundation
1/2 cup black beans, rinsed and drained
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Sauté onion and bell pepper until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add black beans, spinach, and cumin. Stir until spinach is wilted.
Make two wells in the mixture and crack eggs into them.
Cover and cook 4 to 5 minutes until egg whites are set but yolks are still slightly runny.
Season with salt and pepper. Serve with salsa.

Eggs provide cholesterol for testosterone synthesis, choline for brain function, and complete amino acids. Black beans deliver zinc, fiber, and plant protein. Spinach adds magnesium and dietary nitrates. The combination of egg yolk cholesterol with zinc from black beans covers two of the most critical raw materials for testosterone production. A one-pan breakfast that sets a strong hormonal foundation for the day.
Prep/Cook: 25 min | Servings: 1 | Phase: Weeks 5-6, Peak Performance
1 cup cooked brown rice (per package instructions)
Marinate salmon in teriyaki sauce for 10 minutes.
Heat a pan over medium-high heat. Sear salmon skin-side down for 4 minutes.
Flip and cook 3 minutes more until cooked through.
Assemble bowl with brown rice, broccoli, and sliced avocado.
Place salmon on top. Garnish with sesame seeds and green onion.

Wild salmon delivers omega-3 fatty acids and vitamin D, two nutrients with direct clinical links to testosterone production and vascular health. Broccoli provides DIM for estrogen metabolism support. Brown rice supplies magnesium and B vitamins. Avocado adds monounsaturated fats for hormone production. This is a peak-phase bowl that combines the strongest evidence-backed performance foods into a single, complete meal.
Dark Chocolate and Almond Overnight Oats
Prep/Cook: 5 min prep + overnight soak | Servings: 1 | Phase: Weeks 7-8, Lock In
1 tbsp dark chocolate chips (70% cacao or higher)
Combine oats, milk, Greek yogurt, and chia seeds in a jar or container.
Stir well, cover, and refrigerate overnight or at least 6 hours.
In the morning, top with dark chocolate chips, almonds, and honey.
Eat cold or microwave for 1 minute if you prefer it warm.

Dark chocolate with 70% or higher cacao content delivers flavonoids that clinical research has shown to improve endothelial function and blood flow. Almonds provide vitamin E and zinc. Oats contain avenanthramides that support healthy blood pressure and nitric oxide function. Greek yogurt adds protein and
probiotics for gut health and estrogen clearance. This is a Lock In phase breakfast designed to sustain the vascular and hormonal gains built over the previous six weeks.
Blend all ingredients until smooth. Add ice as desired. Consume within 30 minutes of making for best nutrient retention.
Add all ingredients to a blender and blend until smooth.
Maca has solid clinical evidence for improving libido and energy in men. Almond butter provides zinc and healthy fat. Banana delivers potassium and natural sugars to fuel morning performance.
1 small raw beet, peeled and chopped (or 2 oz beet juice)
Blend all ingredients until completely smooth. The beet will create a deep red color.

This is a nitric oxide stacking smoothie. Beet nitrates, pomegranate polyphenols, berry flavonoids, and spinach nitrates all converge in one glass. If you add L-Citrulline powder, you've covered every major NO pathway at once.
1/2 cup whole milk or oat milk
Blend all ingredients until smooth and creamy.

Greek yogurt provides protein and probiotics. Oats give sustained energy without an insulin spike. Ashwagandha reduces cortisol, which competes directly with testosterone. This one sets the hormonal tone for the day.
1 scoop vanilla or chocolate protein powder

Cacao flavanols improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure. This smoothie also makes a solid post-workout recovery drink. The combination of protein, fats, and complex carbs supports muscle repair and testosterone production after training.
Blend watermelon and coconut water first, then add lime, mint, and salt. Blend briefly.

Watermelon is nature's L-Citrulline supplement. The rind has even higher concentrations than the flesh, so blend it in if you don't mind the taste. This is a great pre-activity drink for blood flow and hydration.

Ginger is one of the most studied foods for male fertility and testosterone support. Turmeric with black pepper is one of the most effective anti-inflammatory combinations available. This smoothie targets inflammation and hormonal support simultaneously.

Berry flavonoids protect blood vessel walls and reduce oxidative stress. Pomegranate adds its proven testosterone and blood flow benefits. Flaxseed provides omega-3s and fiber for gut health and estrogen metabolism.
1/2 tsp magnesium glycinate powder or 1 tbsp pumpkin seeds
Blend all ingredients until smooth. Drink 30 to 60 minutes before bed.

Tart cherry juice is one of the few foods with meaningful melatonin content. Magnesium promotes deep, restorative sleep and is directly connected to testosterone production. Since 70 percent of daily T is made during sleep, this drink is doing double duty.
1/2 inch fresh turmeric root or 1/4 tsp turmeric powder
Blend all ingredients until smooth. Strain if preferred.

Vitamin C from citrus supports collagen production, adrenal function, and plays a role in testosterone synthesis. Turmeric and ginger keep inflammation in check. A clean, bright drink to start the morning.
Blend all ingredients until smooth. Add more water to thin if needed.

A pure nitrate and antioxidant delivery system. Dark leafy greens are among the highest dietary sources of nitrates. Lemon adds vitamin C to protect NO from oxidation. Use this when you want a clean, low-sugar option.
Add all ingredients to a blender and blend until smooth.

Watermelon is one of the highest dietary sources of L-Citrulline, an amino acid your body converts directly to arginine and then to nitric oxide. The blood flow benefits are measurable within an hour of consumption. Maca has clinical evidence from multiple randomized trials for improving libido and sexual function in men, operating through a pathway independent of testosterone. This combination delivers both vascular support and libido-specific signaling at the same time.
Add all ingredients to a blender and blend until smooth.

Spinach delivers magnesium, dietary nitrates, and B vitamins, covering blood flow, testosterone production, and energy metabolism in one ingredient. Banana provides potassium, which supports healthy blood pressure and circulation, along with natural sugars for fast-access energy. Greek yogurt adds protein and probiotics for gut and estrogen metabolism support. Chia seeds contribute plant omega-3s and soluble fiber. A clean, complete morning smoothie that covers multiple performance systems without added supplements.

For all infused waters, combine ingredients in a pitcher with 1.5 to 2 liters of filtered water. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours, ideally overnight. Drink throughout the day. Replace ingredients after 24 hours.
Watermelon provides citrulline, lime adds vitamin C, and mint is refreshing without adding sugar. Easy to drink all day.
1/4 tsp ground turmeric or 1/2 inch fresh turmeric root
Combine three of the most effective anti-inflammatory compounds available in a simple drinking water. The lemon also helps alkalize the body and supports liver function.
1/4 cup pomegranate seeds or 2 oz pomegranate juice
Pinch of black pepper (activates polyphenol absorption)
Pomegranate polyphenols protect nitric oxide from oxidative breakdown, keeping it active longer in the body.
Classic and effective. Cucumber is hydrating and mildly detoxifying. Lemon supports liver function. This is the most approachable option for men who are new to infused waters.
1/2 cup mixed blueberries and blackberries, lightly crushed
Berry flavonoids and basil polyphenols provide ongoing antioxidant support throughout the day. The lemon keeps it bright and balanced.
A vitamin C-rich, energizing water that supports immune function and adrenal health. Drink this on high-stress days or when you feel a cold coming on.

Mix coconut water, lemon juice, and sea salt together. Serve chilled or at room temperature. Suitable for any phase as a daily hydration drink, and particularly useful during and after training. Coconut water provides natural potassium and magnesium that support circulation, nerve function, and muscle contraction. Lemon adds vitamin C, which protects nitric oxide from oxidative breakdown. The pinch of sea salt replaces sodium lost during training and prevents electrolyte imbalance that can reduce stamina and focus.

Each shot is 1 to 3 oz and is meant to be consumed quickly. Use a small blender, juicer, or shake vigorously in a jar. These are concentrated delivery systems, so the ingredients are potent. Start with once daily and adjust based on how your body responds.
1/2 inch fresh ginger, juiced or grated fine
Take 30 to 60 minutes before exercise or intimacy for maximum blood flow effect. The beet nitrates convert to NO most efficiently in this window.
1 oz fresh ginger juice or 1 inch ginger blended with 1 oz water
Ginger is one of the most studied botanicals for testosterone support. The cayenne improves circulation. Take first thing in the morning before eating.
1/2 tsp turmeric powder or 1 inch fresh turmeric, juiced
Pinch of black pepper (essential for curcumin absorption)
1 tsp coconut oil or olive oil (fat increases absorption)
Curcumin in turmeric is a powerful anti-inflammatory compound. The black pepper and fat are non-negotiable for absorption. Without them, very little curcumin makes it into your bloodstream.
1 clove raw garlic, minced or pressed
Raw garlic contains allicin, which supports testosterone and cardiovascular health. Apple cider vinegar supports insulin sensitivity. Not the most pleasant shot, but the results speak for themselves.
A natural energy activator without caffeine. Cayenne improves circulation and thermogenesis. Honey provides quick fuel. Take mid-afternoon when energy dips.
Three of the most clinically supported ingredients for male sexual health in one concentrated shot. Make a batch and refrigerate for up to three days.
Apple cider vinegar supports gut health, blood sugar regulation, and liver function. Take before a meal to support digestion and metabolic health.

Ashwagandha is most effective when taken consistently. This shot format makes it easy to take without capsules. Best taken in the evening to support cortisol reduction and overnight testosterone production.

Stir ashwagandha powder into hot water until fully dissolved. Sweeten with honey if desired. Drink warm in the mid-afternoon, ideally a few hours before your evening capsule dose if you are on the supplement stack. This tea format is a direct alternative to the capsule form and delivers the same cortisol-reducing benefits. The mechanism is covered in Chapter 24: ashwagandha lowers the cortisol that competes with and suppresses testosterone production. Consistent use over eight weeks is where the clinical evidence is strongest.
1 cup (8 oz) 100 percent unsweetened pomegranate juice, chilled

Take one cup mid-morning as a standalone drink, between Weeks 3 and 8. Choose 100 percent pure pomegranate juice with no added sugar or blends. Pomegranate juice has some of the most compelling clinical evidence of any food for male sexual health, including a randomized controlled trial showing measurable increases in testosterone and improvements in blood flow after 14 days of daily use. The evidence for this is covered in Chapter 4. This can also be used as the base for the Morning Testosterone Shot already in this section, replacing or supplementing the 2 oz component with a full glass for a higher-dose option on training days.

The right kitchen setup isn't about being a chef. It's about removing friction from healthy eating so that making the right choice is easier than making the wrong one. The equipment in this chapter directly supports the meal plan, recipes, smoothies, and shots in this guide.

Amazon affiliate links are included for convenience. If you already own a version of something on this list, keep it. These recommendations are for men starting from scratch or looking to upgrade specific pieces.
Note: Replace YOUR_TAG-20 in the companion app and spreadsheet with your Amazon affiliate tag before sharing links. Sign up at affiliate-program.amazon.com.

Non-negotiable for daily smoothies, shots, and the NO Rocket blend. A weak blender will not fully process beets, ginger, or leafy greens. Ninja or Vitamix are the go-to options at different price points.

Cold-press juicers extract significantly more juice from beets, ginger, and greens than centrifugal models, with higher nutrient retention. For the beet juice shots and NO Rocket, this is the best tool.
A faster, more affordable alternative to masticating. Works well for beets, citrus, and ginger. Less efficient on leafy greens. Good starting point before investing in a cold press.
Steaming preserves more nutrients than boiling. Used for asparagus, broccoli, and other performance vegetables in the meal plan.

The best surface for searing steak, salmon, and chicken thighs. Retains heat better than anything else and adds trace dietary iron to your food. A 10 or 12-inch pan handles every recipe in this guide.

Dramatically cuts meal prep time for batch cooking chicken, beef, and stews. Set it and walk away. One cook session can fuel three to four days of meals. Instant Pots usually have a rice cooker mode. Quinoa mode is a plus.
Produces crispy chicken, roasted vegetables, and reheated meals without excess oil. Faster than the oven for small quantities. A strong second cooking method for busy nights.
Tracks weight, body fat percentage, muscle mass, BMI, and water percentage. Syncs with your phone for trend tracking. Critical for monitoring body composition changes during the plan.
Accurate portioning for protein and supplements. Especially useful for measuring L-Citrulline and creatine powder dosing without guessing.
For L-Citrulline, creatine, and collagen powder mixing. Also useful for beet root powder drinks on the go. Get at least two so one is always clean.
Running Stack A through E means multiple supplements per day across multiple timing windows. A weekly organizer with AM and PM compartments turns a complicated routine into a five-second daily habit.
Meal prep without plastic. Microwave safe, oven safe, stackable, and do not leach BPA. Get a variety pack with lids. These replace plastic food storage containers completely.
Reusable, freezer safe, and plastic-free for storing prepped ingredients, marinating meats, and packing snacks. Good complement to glass containers.
Glass or Stainless Steel Water Bottle

You will be drinking a lot of water on this plan, including infused performance waters. Glass bottles and Stainless Steel do not leach chemicals, keep water tasting clean, and hold infused water without staining.
Portioned meal prep containers for prepping lunches and dinners in advance. Look for BPA-free options with secure-locking lids. Having five days of meals ready is the most underrated adherence tool.
Accurate portioning for recipes, supplements, and shots. Stainless steel sets last indefinitely.
Oysters are one of the most testosterone-supportive foods in the guide. If you plan to eat fresh oysters regularly, a proper shucker and protective glove make this safe and straightforward.
Handheld blender for soups, sauces, and quick single-serving smoothies without pulling out the full blender. Useful for the ginger warrior and other shot-adjacent drinks.
For green tea, ginger tea, and warm pre-bed drinks. Fast, precise temperature control makes a real difference in green tea extraction and taste.
Bamboo or Stainless Steel Cutting Boards
Dedicated boards for meat, produce, and fish keep your prep hygienic. Bamboo is durable, knife-friendly, and naturally antimicrobial. Stainless steel boards are easy to sanitize and last a lifetime.
For printing before, during, and after photos to paste into your journal section. Also useful for printing your supplement schedule or shopping list to keep on the fridge.

Since vascular health and blood flow are central to this plan, monitoring your blood pressure at home gives you a direct, measurable feedback loop. Aim for readings below 120/80. Track weekly during the 8-week plan.
Required for the Perfect Stack App, supplement and meal tracking, workout logging, taking photos, and accessing affiliate product links. Already in your pocket.

Priority guide: Essential items should be in your kitchen before you start Week 1. Highly Recommended items improve consistency and results meaningfully. Optional items are useful additions for men who want to go deeper.

The Perfect Stack is a structured, layered supplement protocol designed to fill the gaps that diet and lifestyle alone can't always close. It isn't a replacement for eating well and training hard. It's a multiplier. The stack is organized into functional categories so you can understand what each piece does and why it belongs.
Chapter 9: Why Supplements Matter More as You Age
The Gap Between What You Need and What You Get

Modern food is less nutritious than it used to be. Soil depletion, long transport times, and food processing all strip nutrients from the food supply. At the same time, the demands on your body increase as you age. Hormone production requires raw materials. Vascular health requires specific micronutrients. Recovery requires compounds that become harder to produce endogenously as the years go by.

Supplementation is how you close that gap. Not randomly, and not by buying everything the supplement industry tells you to buy. Deliberately, with an understanding of what each compound does, why your body needs it, and at what dose it's actually effective.

The supplements in this stack have been selected based on the quality of the research behind them, the specific needs of men over 35, and the way they complement each other. Some are fundamental and should be taken daily by virtually every man in this age group. Others are targeted for specific goals or situations.
What to Look for in Supplement Quality
Third-party testing is non-negotiable. Look for NSF Certified for Sport, Informed Sport, or USP verification. These confirm the product contains what it says and nothing it shouldn't.

Form matters. Magnesium glycinate absorbs significantly better than magnesium oxide. Vitamin D3 is more effective than D2. Methylated B vitamins work better for men who have MTHFR gene variants. The details are in the label.
Dose matters. Many products contain ingredients at levels far below what research identifies as effective. Read the actual amounts, not just the ingredient list.

The Perfect Stack Interactive Spreadsheet lets you build your custom stack, compare specific recommended products, and calculate your monthly cost. It accounts for your age, goals, and any medications you're taking. See the Closing section for more.

The Perfect Stack is organized into five named stacks. Each stack has a primary objective, and they're designed to layer on top of each other. Most men will run Stack A continuously and add from there based on their specific goals.

In the Perfect Stack Spreadsheet and App, these stack labels correspond to the stack-building modules. When you see Stack B referenced in the guide, you can find the exact product recommendations, dosing logic, and cost breakdown for that stack in the companion tools.
Fill critical micronutrient gaps. Baseline hormonal and cardiovascular support.

Complete Structured Supplement Protocol combining A through D with longevity support.
Men running the full 8-week plan or the 8-Week Performance Reset.
Stack A is the platform everything else runs on. Don't skip to Stack C or D without Stack A firmly in place. The advanced stacks are multipliers, not substitutes.

These are the non-negotiable baseline supplements. Most men in their mid-30s and older are deficient in several of these, and even mild deficiencies in any of them can measurably reduce testosterone, energy, and performance.

Choose a men's formula. Thorne Men's Multi 50+ or Micro Ingredients Methylated are well-regarded options.
Testosterone support, bone health, immune function

Take with a fat-containing meal. K2 (MK-7 form) directs calcium to bones, not arteries.

200 mg once daily, evening, 30-60 minutes before bed.
Glycinate form is best absorbed and gentlest on the stomach. Avoid magnesium oxide.

Often missing from multivitamins. Confirmed role in vitamin D metabolism. Early research suggests it may support free testosterone by reducing SHBG; evidence base is preliminary.
2,000 mg EPA+DHA once daily. Take with largest meal.
Triglyceride form absorbs best. Look for molecularly distilled for purity.
Testosterone synthesis, immune function, sperm quality
15-30 mg daily. Take AM with food.
Zinc picolinate or bisglycinate absorbs well. Don't exceed 40 mg long-term without monitoring copper levels.
As directed. Take AM with breakfast.
Methylfolate and methylcobalamin (B12) forms bypass MTHFR gene variants that affect a significant portion of men.
100-200 mcg daily. Take AM or midday with food.
Brazil nuts are a food source (1-2 per day). Don't exceed 400 mcg from all sources.

Always select a zinc supplement dosed at 15mg or less per serving. Higher-dose zinc products (25mg or above) combined with a multivitamin can easily exceed the 40mg daily tolerable upper limit established by the NIH, which causes copper deficiency and immune dysfunction over time. The Perfect Stack recommended zinc product is Pure Encapsulations Zinc 15mg.

Note on Stack A multivitamins and B vitamins: A multivitamin and a methylated B-complex are not the same supplement and are not interchangeable. A multivitamin covers broad micronutrient gaps across vitamins and minerals. A methylated B-complex provides B vitamins in their active, bioavailable forms, including methylfolate (active B9), methylcobalamin (active B12), and pyridoxal-5-phosphate (active B6). Approximately 30-40% of men carry MTHFR variants that impair conversion of standard B vitamins to their active forms. For these men, standard multivitamin B content is largely ineffective for neurotransmitter synthesis, methylation, and homocysteine regulation. Both supplements serve distinct roles. Take them together.
Important: Multivitamin and Stack Supplement Interactions

The three recommended multivitamin options each contain meaningful amounts of zinc, selenium, magnesium, vitamin D, and B vitamins. When taking any of these multivitamins, the doses of certain additional supplements in your stack are automatically adjusted in the Perfect Stack app and Interactive Spreadsheet to prevent exceeding established daily upper limits. These adjustments include:
• Vitamin D3+K2: reduced based on your multivitamin’s vitamin D content
• Selenium: not recommended when any of the three multivitamins is selected, as they each provide the full daily amount
• Zinc: reduced or not recommended depending on your multivitamin’s zinc content
• Magnesium: reduced when using Thorne Men’s Multi 50+ which provides 180mg
• Methylated B-Complex: not recommended when using Life Extension Two-Per-Day due to its high B6 content
• Vitamin C: reduced when using Life Extension Two-Per-Day

These adjustments are calculated automatically. If you are following the guide without the app or spreadsheet, consult your healthcare provider about appropriate doses given your multivitamin selection.

The Perfect Stack app performs these adjustments automatically and in real time. When you select your multivitamin in the Stack Builder, the app cross-references every other supplement in your stack against the nutrient content of your chosen multivitamin and applies NIH Tolerable Upper Intake Level calculations to identify any combinations that would exceed established safe daily limits.
The following adjustments are applied automatically based on multivitamin selection:

With Thorne Men’s Multi 50+: Vitamin D3 reduced to 2,000 IU (Thorne provides 1,000 IU; combined 5,000 IU would exceed the 4,000 IU upper limit). Zinc not recommended (Thorne provides 30mg; combined 45mg exceeds the 40mg upper limit). Magnesium reduced to 150mg (Thorne provides 180mg, keeping supplemental total below 350mg). Selenium not recommended (Thorne provides the full 200mcg daily amount).
With Micro Ingredients Methylated Multi: Vitamin D3 reduced to 2,000 IU. Zinc reduced to 10mg. Selenium not recommended.

With Life Extension Two-Per-Day: Vitamin D3 reduced to 1,000 IU (Life Extension provides 2,000 IU). Methylated B-Complex not recommended (Life Extension provides 75mg B6; additional B-Complex would exceed the 100mg upper limit associated with peripheral neuropathy risk). Vitamin C reduced to 500mg. Zinc reduced to 5mg. Selenium not recommended.

These adjustments are applied silently in the background. The Stack Builder displays the adjusted dose with a strikethrough of the original dose so you can see exactly what changed and why. No action is required on your part - the app handles the calculations automatically.
If you are following the protocol without the app, refer to the multivitamin safety note above and consult your healthcare provider about appropriate doses for your chosen multivitamin.

Timing shown is the general recommendation. The Stack Builder in the Perfect Stack app at getperfectstack.com automatically calculates personalized doses based on your health profile, medications, and goals.
Vitamin D: The Testosterone Vitamin Most Men Are Missing

Vitamin D insufficiency is common, with NHANES data showing approximately 25 to 41 percent of American men with suboptimal levels depending on the threshold used and the population studied. Higher rates occur in winter months, northern latitudes, and men with limited sun exposure. What most men don't know is that vitamin D functions more like a hormone than a vitamin, and its receptors are found in virtually every tissue in the body, including the Leydig cells that produce testosterone.

Studies show men with adequate vitamin D levels consistently have higher testosterone than deficient men. Most physicians target 40 to 60 ng/mL for hormonal and immune optimization; clinical sufficiency begins at 30 ng/mL. Supplementing to restore levels in genuinely deficient men has been shown to meaningfully increase testosterone. Getting your D level tested is one of the most worthwhile things you can do before building your stack.
Magnesium: The Mineral That Unlocks Your Testosterone

Magnesium is involved in over 300 enzymatic reactions in the body. For men specifically, it plays a critical role in testosterone availability. Magnesium competes with SHBG for testosterone binding. When magnesium levels are adequate, more free testosterone remains available for your tissues to use. When magnesium is low, SHBG binds more, and your effective testosterone drops regardless of what the total number looks like.

Most men are marginally deficient. Intense exercise, stress, and alcohol all deplete magnesium further. Supplementing with 200 mg of magnesium glycinate once daily, in the evening 30 to 60 minutes before bed, also improves sleep depth and quality, which feeds back directly into testosterone production overnight.
Chapter 12: Stack B = A+B: Testosterone Support Stack

Stack B targets the hormonal axis directly. These supplements work through three mechanisms: reducing cortisol to free up hormonal capacity, stimulating the signaling pathway that tells your testes to produce more testosterone, and inhibiting the enzymes that convert testosterone to estrogen or DHT. They build on Stack A as your non-negotiable baseline.
Cortisol reduction, testosterone support, stress resilience
600 mg once daily. Take PM in the evening.
KSM-66 is the most studied extract. Lowers cortisol by up to 30%. Testosterone increases of 15-17% documented in younger men and men with infertility; directionally consistent in older men.
Testosterone support, libido, free T availability
200-400 mg standardized extract (1:200) daily. Take AM or midday; avoid evening.
Increases free and total testosterone in 5 published RCTs (moderate, consistent evidence). Works through enzyme activity in the testosterone biosynthesis pathway, not an LH stimulator.
500-600 mg daily. Take AM with food.
May inhibit enzymes that convert testosterone to estrogen and DHT, increasing free T availability. In vitro data is strong; human aromatase data is mixed.
25-50 mg daily (over 40 only, test first). Take PM with evening meal.
Converts to testosterone and estrogen. Test baseline DHEA-S before supplementing. Not for men under 35.
600 mg daily. Take AM with food. Cycle 8 weeks on, 4 weeks off.

Start with 300mg (1 capsule) daily for the first week to assess individual tolerance before increasing to the full 600mg dose. Do not exceed 900mg per day. Limited human trial data. Animal studies show testosterone-elevating effects via LH stimulation. The same animal studies at high doses showed potential testicular toxicity, making cycling and conservative dosing essential. Avoid if you have testicular health concerns. Due to limited long-term human safety data, cycle Fadogia Agrestis 8 weeks on, 4 weeks off. Considered emerging and promising but requires more
human research before being classified as well-established.
Cortisol reduction, HPG axis protection, sleep quality
100-200 mg daily. Take AM for focus; PM for sleep support.

An amino acid found naturally in green tea. Reduces cortisol and psychological stress without causing sedation. Works synergistically with caffeine to produce calm, focused energy without the jittery effect. Pairs well with Ashwagandha for comprehensive cortisol management. Well tolerated with no known significant interactions at standard doses.

Timing shown is the general recommendation. The Stack Builder in the Perfect Stack app at getperfectstack.com automatically calculates personalized doses based on your health profile, medications, and goals.

⚠ DHEA is a hormone precursor and shouldn't be taken without knowing your baseline levels. In men with already-high estrogen, DHEA can worsen the imbalance. Discuss with your doctor or order a baseline DHEA-S test before starting.
The Tongkat Ali and Ashwagandha Combination

These two adaptogens work through complementary mechanisms and combine well. Ashwagandha reduces cortisol, which directly frees up the hormonal capacity for testosterone production. Tongkat Ali supports testosterone through direct enzyme-level activity in the biosynthesis pathway, increasing both total and free testosterone independent of LH signaling. Together they address both the suppressor, which is cortisol, and the production pathway directly.

Clinical trials on each individually have shown measurable increases in total and free testosterone in men with suboptimal levels. Neither is a substitute for TRT when testosterone is genuinely clinically low, but for men in the functional but suboptimal range, this combination is one of the most effective natural interventions available.
Chapter 13: Stack C = A+B+C: Nitric Performance Stack

Stack C specifically targets nitric oxide production, vascular performance, and energy. These supplements work best when combined with the foundation stack and the dietary approach in Part 2, and they're timed around physical activity and intimacy for maximum effect.
Nitric oxide production, blood flow, erectile quality
6 g once daily. Take 45-90 min pre-activity. Note: reduce to 3 g if on PDE5 inhibitor.
More effective than L-Arginine for sustained NO elevation. Citrulline Malate 2:1 is a popular form.
Dietary nitrate source, blood flow, exercise performance
500 mg standardized extract or 2 oz beet juice. Take 60-90 min before activity.

Take 60-90 min before activity for peak effect. Well-validated for athletic performance and blood pressure. Same vascular mechanisms are relevant to erectile function, though direct ED-specific trials are limited. Stack with L-Citrulline for synergistic NO support.
Mitochondrial energy, cardiovascular health, sperm quality
100mg once daily with a fat-containing meal
Ubiquinol form is more bioavailable for men over 40. CoQ10 declines with age and statin use.
500-1,000 mg daily. Take AM with food.
Protects nitric oxide from oxidative breakdown, keeping it active longer. Take with meals.
500-1,000 mg daily. Take AM with food.
Flavonoid found in onions and apples. Synergizes with vitamin C. Improves endothelial cell function.
100-200 mg daily. Take AM with food.

One of the most clinically validated supplements for erectile function. A published RCT showed that combining Pycnogenol with L-Arginine (and by extension L-Citrulline) significantly improved erectile function scores in men with mild to moderate ED. Works by increasing eNOS activity, the enzyme that produces nitric oxide in blood vessel walls. Also a potent antioxidant that
protects NO from oxidative breakdown. Take with meals.

Timing shown is the general recommendation. The Stack Builder in the Perfect Stack app at getperfectstack.com automatically calculates personalized doses based on your health profile, medications, and goals.

For years, L-Arginine was marketed as the go-to supplement for nitric oxide and sexual performance. The problem is that orally consumed L-Arginine is largely broken down in the gut and liver before it reaches the bloodstream. L-Citrulline bypasses this and converts to L-Arginine more efficiently in the kidneys, leading to higher and more sustained blood arginine and NO levels than direct arginine supplementation.

The research is consistent on this. L-Citrulline supplementation improves erectile function, reduces blood pressure, and enhances exercise performance with good tolerability. Stacking it with beet root or beet juice creates a dual-pathway approach that covers both enzymatic NO production and dietary nitrate conversion.
Chapter 14: Stack D = A+B+C+D: Libido Amplifier Stack

Stack D targets sexual drive independently of testosterone levels. The supplements in this stack don't directly raise testosterone or nitric oxide. Instead, they work through separate pathways to improve libido, mood, and sexual function. This makes Stack D relevant for men whose drive is low even when T levels are within range.
1,000 mg once daily. Take AM with a meal.
Doesn't directly raise testosterone but reliably improves libido and sexual function independently. Well tolerated.
200-400 mg standardized (min 3% ginsenosides) daily. Take AM or midday; avoid evening.
One of the few botanicals with direct evidence for improving erectile function in clinical trials.
Natural PDE5 inhibition, libido, testosterone support
500 mg once daily. Take AM with food.

Icariin, the active compound in Epimedium, is a natural PDE5 inhibitor, though significantly weaker than pharmaceutical options like tadalafil. Several studies show improvement in erectile function and libido. Best used as part of a complete stack rather than as a standalone intervention. Caution: due to mild PDE5 activity, men on nitrate medications should avoid. Men on prescription PDE5 inhibitors should consult their physician before adding.

Timing shown is the general recommendation. The Stack Builder in the Perfect Stack app at getperfectstack.com automatically calculates personalized doses based on your health profile, medications, and goals.
Why These Work When Testosterone Is Normal

Libido isn't governed solely by testosterone. Dopamine, endorphins, adrenal health, and neurological sensitivity all play a role. Maca root has been shown in randomized controlled trials to improve sexual desire independent of changes in testosterone or estrogen. Panax Ginseng has direct clinical evidence for improving erectile function, with its active compounds, ginsenosides, acting on both the nervous system and vascular smooth muscle.

Men who have optimized everything else and still find libido lagging often respond well to Stack D. It's also a useful addition for men on TRT whose T numbers look good but whose drive hasn't fully returned.

Stack E is the Ultimate Protocol. It combines everything in Stacks A through D and adds longevity support, the supplements that protect and extend the system you're building. These address cellular energy production, metabolic health, prostate function, gut integrity, and connective tissue repair. They may not produce dramatic short-term effects, but their cumulative impact on how you feel and function at 55, 65, and beyond is significant.
250-500 mg NMN or 300 mg NR daily. Take AM (morning preferred for energy effect).

NAD+ declines sharply with age. Early human evidence confirms these precursors raise NAD+ levels in blood and muscle. Long-term outcome data is still accumulating; the longevity case is supported by strong animal research.
250-500 mg daily (liposomal or with piperine). Take PM with a fat-containing meal.

Activates sirtuin pathways associated with longevity. Important: standard resveratrol has poor bioavailability (~0.5-2%). Choose liposomal, micronized, or piperine-enhanced forms for meaningful absorption. Synergizes with NMN.
Blood sugar regulation, metabolic health, gut microbiome
500 mg twice daily with meals. Split AM and PM.

Has performed comparably to Metformin in several small, short-term trials, primarily in Asian populations. Very promising for metabolic and insulin health. Western long-term data is limited but directionally consistent.
320 mg liposterolic extract once daily. Take AM with fat-containing meal.

Evidence is mixed. A 27-RCT Cochrane review found no benefit over placebo for urinary symptoms; some smaller trials suggest modest benefit for prostate comfort. Include cautiously if prostate symptom relief is a goal, with realistic expectations.
10-30 mg daily. Take AM or PM with food.
Found in cooked tomatoes. Supplementation or consistent tomato consumption associated with lower prostate cancer risk.
Gut health, immune function, estrogen clearance
Multi-strain, 10-50 billion CFU daily. Take AM on empty stomach or PM before bed.
A healthy gut microbiome improves estrogen metabolism and reduces systemic inflammation. Refrigerated strains preferred.
Muscle strength, cognitive function, cellular energy
3-5 g daily. Timing is flexible; consistency matters more than the window.
One of the most studied supplements in existence. Improves strength, power, and recovery. Also supports brain function.
Joint health, tendon strength, skin integrity
10-15 g daily. Take PM before bed with vitamin C.
Particularly useful for men doing heavy training. Supports connective tissue repair and recovery.

Timing shown is the general recommendation. The Stack Builder in the Perfect Stack app at getperfectstack.com automatically calculates personalized doses based on your health profile, medications, and goals.

Creatine has been studied for decades and consistently shows benefits that go well beyond gym performance. It improves strength and power output, supports cognitive function, reduces fatigue, and plays a role in cellular energy production. The safety profile is excellent, and it's one of the most cost-effective supplements in the entire stack.

The stigma around creatine as a "gym bro supplement" is outdated. Research in aging populations shows it helps preserve muscle mass and cognitive function over time. For men over 40, it's one of the most worthwhile additions to a daily routine.

The Perfect Stack app includes an automated Stack Safety Check that runs every time you build or update your stack. It analyzes every combination of supplements in your selected stacks and flags known interactions, timing conflicts, and nutrient overlaps.
Conflict (red): supplement combinations that exceed established safety limits or have significant contraindications. The app automatically adjusts or removes the conflicting supplement.
Caution (yellow): combinations that are generally safe but require attention to timing, dosing, or individual health factors.
Note (blue): combinations with synergistic effects or timing recommendations that optimize results.

Each flagged supplement shows a pulsing colored dot next to its name in the Stack Builder. Clicking the dot scrolls to the detailed explanation. The Perfect Stack app and Interactive Spreadsheet currently track 30 supplement interactions across the full Stack A through E protocol. The interaction count summary is visible to Foundation subscribers. Full interaction details and recommendations are available in the Ultimate Protocol.
Ashwagandha and Tongkat Ali cortisol pathway overlap
Tongkat Ali and Fadogia Agrestis androgenic amplification
L-Citrulline and Beet Root synergistic nitric oxide pairing
NMN and Resveratrol NAD+ pathway synergy

Berberine + Quercetin (caution): Both activate AMPK and modulate blood glucose pathways. Combined use may cause additive glucose-lowering effects that could cause hypoglycemia in diabetic or pre-diabetic men or those on glucose-lowering medications. Monitor blood sugar closely if combining both. Take at different times of day to reduce overlap.
Resveratrol + Omega-3 (caution): Both have antiplatelet properties. Combined use increases bleeding risk for men on blood thinners or NSAIDs.
Horny Goat Weed + Omega-3 (caution): Icariin in Horny Goat Weed has antiplatelet properties similar to Omega-3. Combined use may increase bleeding risk.
Fenugreek + DHEA (caution): Both influence androgen pathways. Combined use may amplify androgenic effects. Monitor for acne, hair thinning, or elevated PSA.
Panax Ginseng + Tongkat Ali (caution): Both stimulate LH production. Combined use may amplify androgenic signaling. Monitor for elevated heart rate or agitation.
Saw Palmetto + Zinc (caution): Both inhibit 5-alpha reductase. Combined use amplifies DHT reduction. Monitor libido or sexual function.
CoQ10 + Berberine (caution): Berberine may reduce CoQ10 synthesis. Taking CoQ10 alongside Berberine is recommended to offset this effect.
Probiotic + Berberine (note): Berberine has antimicrobial properties that can reduce probiotic effectiveness. Take at least 2 hours apart.
Lycopene + Vitamin C (note): Vitamin C enhances lycopene bioavailability. Take together with a fat-containing meal.
Quercetin + Vitamin C (note): Vitamin C regenerates oxidized quercetin. A well-established synergistic pairing.
NMN + Vitamin D3 (note): Both support mitochondrial function through complementary pathways. Take together in the morning.
Collagen + Vitamin C (note): Vitamin C is required for collagen synthesis. Take together for optimal collagen production.
Multivitamin nutrient duplication across Stacks A through E
Access the Stack Safety Check at getperfectstack.com after building your stack.

When you take your supplements matters almost as much as what you take. Some require food for absorption. Some compete with each other for uptake. Some work best when cortisol is naturally lower, in the evening. The schedule below is organized by timing window and mapped to each stack.
This is the practical daily operating schedule for the Ultimate Protocol (Stack E). If you're running a partial stack, simply take the supplements that apply to you at the window indicated.

Always use the app or spreadsheet for your personalized protocol.
